column1: SSO | DBeaver MFA with CWA is not working
column36:
h3. Impact

The bug affects users trying to use DBeaver with Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) through CWA. Users are unable to proceed after successful MFA, which hinders their ability to gain approval.

h3. Expected behaviour

After successful MFA, the system should allow users to move ahead for approval.

h3. Actual behaviour

After successful MFA, the system does not proceed for approval.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Attempt to use DBeaver with MFA enabled.
* Complete the MFA process using CWA.
* Observe that the system does not move ahead for approval.

h3. Other information

CWA and CMFA are working as expected.

column1: PSS IAM | Reconcilled DI mapping with HI
column36:
Dear Team,

We have Microsoft AD as a target application for our customer and we have to perform the full recon scan to pull all the DI from AD and assign/map these DI to existing HI based on the attribute UserID.

but the userlogon name in AD attribute is not same as userID in HI.

the userID has partial value of userlogon name.



eg : userID = 1010

user logon name = AD1010.

column402:
04-16-2026 4:44 PM;712020:f3fc300f-b098-4580-af05-3d32bcb92657;Dear @Aniket Maurya,

As discussed, we have a concern regarding the transition from Excel-based onboarding to Oracle DB as the Source of Truth (SOT). Specifically, after onboarding users using Excel, it is not confirmed whether configuring Oracle DB as SOT later will correctly link to the existing users or attempt to create new users again with a different USERID (e.g., using EMP_ID such as 1010).

This concern remains even when EMP_ID is maintained as a common/primary attribute across both sources.

To validate this behavior, It was requested to perform a UAT test in our internal environment and confirm how the system handles this transition.

@Janam BhavsarĀ¢ Since the Oracle DB SOT environment has already been set up by the connector team for testing, could you please provide us access or assign someone who can assist us for approximately 30 minutes to perform this validation?

This will help us confirm the behavior and finalize the approach accordingly.

Looking forward to your support.

04-10-2026 4:56 PM;712020:7d7105fc-4c14-401f-b04b-e8703889b7b0;Connected with [~accountid:6242d8b67a3f9e006ab10a36] regarding this issue. After reviewing the API bot logs, HI, DI, and asset configurations, it was found that in the asset the username was set to "Admin Set username", while the username field was not configured for the DIs. This caused the username to be empty, leading to API errors.

Since the reactivation call was being triggered for users, IÆve asked [~accountid:6242d8b67a3f9e006ab10a36]  to create a new asset so that the user creation flow is executed again. Please ensure the Username is set to "arcon set username".

[~accountid:6242d8b67a3f9e006ab10a36] , please go ahead and test the JMLR operations. Also, make sure to follow the steps outlined in the SOPs for asset configurations, which will prevent this issue from occurring again.

column1: Error message displayed in terminal when selecting No for ōAllow with MFA and Approvalö command
column36:
When executing a command configured with *Allow with MFA and Approval*, if the user selects *No* in the approval prompt, an unexpected *error message is displayed in the terminal session*. Instead of a proper denial message, the system displays an unrelated error in the SSH session.

!bandicam 2026-03-06 21-48-34-961.mp4|width=320,alt="bandicam 2026-03-06 21-48-34-961.mp4"!

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to the *Workspace*.
# Initiate an *SSH connection* to the Linux asset.
# Execute a command configured with *Allow with MFA and Approval*.
# Wait for the *approval/MFA prompt*.
# Click *No* on the approval request.
# Observe the message displayed in the terminal.

*Expected Result:*
When *No* is selected, the command execution should be *denied gracefully* with a clear message such as _ōCommand execution deniedö_.

*Actual Result:*
An *unexpected error message is displayed in the terminal* instead of a proper command denial message.

column328:
+*Behavior of 10.10.1.104*+
We have recorded a video evidence of a peculiar behavior for the target server that we are trying to connect and do an MFA (This behavior can or cannot be observed by any target server).
In the video, we are trying to connect to 10.10.1.104 using normal PUTTY.exe. As you see in the video we have successfully connected to 10.10.1.104. After successful connection we have tried to execute a command which is not valid (eg: ate). After entering this command the putty rightly shows "Command not found" and post that it shows the below error:

"Failed to search for [file: Faile|file: Fail]d to download gpg key for repo 'baseos': Curl error (35): SSL connect error for [+https://download.rockylinux.org/pub/rocky/RPM-GPG-KEY-rockyofficial+|https://download.rockylinux.org/pub/rocky/RPM-GPG-KEY-rockyofficial] [OpenSSL SSL_connect: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to [+download.rockylinux.org:443+|http://download.rockylinux.org:443/] ]"

This error clearly shows and satisfies the fact that this is a behavior peculiar to how 10.10.1.104 server is reacting. There is nothing that we can do for the above error message.
column340:
Carriage Return tag to be used
Whenever anyone wants to truncate or stop the execution of a command on putty we press "Ctrl + C" command which truncates the execution of the command and takes the user to a new line to execute the next command.
When we programmatically try to mimic the above, we just add "Ctrl + C" programmatically and then the target server auto adds the enter key. But there are some target servers who behave differently in which when we enter "Ctrl + C" programmatically they do not auto add the enter. So, to cater to such variety of target server we make use of <carriagereturn> tag which programmatically informs the target server to add an enter after the "Ctrl + C". 

So, the solution to this observation put by the QA team is to make use of <carriagereturn> tag. We would request the QA Team to make a note of this and use this for future occurring similar cases.

column1: User able to login on second attempt after entering incorrect password when TOTP authentication is enabled
column36:
When *TOTP authentication is enabled*, it is entering an *incorrect password during the first login attempt*, the system should validate the credentials properly before allowing access. However, the system allows the *user to log in successfully on the second attempt without proper validation*, which indicates an issue in the authentication validation flow.

!bandicam 2026-03-06 19-09-59-118.mp4|width=591,alt="bandicam 2026-03-06 19-09-59-118.mp4"!

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Ensure *TOTP authentication is enabled* for the user/Digital Identity.
# Initiate *SSO login*.
# Enter an *incorrect password* in the first attempt.
# Attempt to login again.
# Observe the login behavior.

*Expected Result:*
If the *password is incorrect*, the system should *not allow login* until the correct credentials are entered and properly validated.

*Actual Result:*
Even after entering an *incorrect password in the first attempt*, the *user is able to login successfully on the second attempt*, indicating improper authentication validation.

column1: Session Lockout and Reconnect Not Working on Mac Thick Client
column36:
The SSO session on the *MAC Thick Client* does not lock out according to the configured *idle session lockout time* set in the Admin Console. As a result, the session continues to run instead of getting locked after the idle timeout period.

Additionally, when the session is disconnected, the *Reconnect* option does not appear, preventing the user from resuming the session.
column328:
Earlier this feature was not available for mac thick client ARMH. So even if session timeout value is set at the server side and if session is idle for that much time, user was still able to access the launched service.

Now session will get locked if the session is idle for the selected time.
column340: Implemented the session timeout feature. Now session will get locked if the session is idle for the selected time.
column389:
- Test only for global settings: Settings Page > Application Configuration > Set session lock
- Take ssh linux/ windows rdp session
- keep it idle for the time which is set in application configuration of settings page.
- Authentication pop up should come after the selected time if session is idle.
- Now user should not be able to perform any activity or execute any command on the launched service
- Enter password and click on "Submit" btn
- Three attempts are given to enter correct password. After three unsuccessful attempts session should terminate.
- If password is correct, session resumes.

- Also, if user doesn't know the password. It should click on "Terminate Session" btn. This will terminate the session.
- If the user clicks on red cross (close btn) of the Auth window session should terminate.

column1: Session Lockout and Reconnect Not Working on Ubuntu Thick Client
column36:
The SSO session on the *Ubuntu Thick Client* does not lock out according to the configured *idle session lockout time* set in the Admin Console. As a result, the session continues to run instead of getting locked after the idle timeout period.

Additionally, when the session is disconnected, the *Reconnect* option does not appear, preventing the user from resuming the session.

Furthermore, in the Ubuntu Thick Client for *CI*, the application title is displayed as *ōARCON PAMö* at the top. It should instead be displayed as *ōARCON CI.ö*

column1: Domain User Re-Login Fails After Session Timeout During SSO on RHEL Environment
column36:
When performing SSO on the RHEL environment using a *Domain User*, the session works initially. However, once the session time expires, a popup window appears prompting the user to re-login by entering the password.

For *Domain Users*, this re-authentication fails and throws an error (as shown in the attached evidence ¢ ōException at Session unlock. Session will be terminated. Index was outside the bounds of the array.ö).

For *Normal (non-domain) users*, the re-login process after session timeout works as expected.

This indicates the issue is specific to Domain User authentication during session unlock in the RHEL environment.

column328:
While troubleshooting it was observed that the TINYINT(1) datatype in the MySQL database was behaving different for windows hosting & linux hosting. Below are their database values and their equivalent application layer values (C#).

For Windows: 

* DB-Value: 1, C#-Value: 1
* DB-Value: 0, C#-Value: 0

For Linux:

* DB-Value: 0. C#-Value: False
* *DB-Vaule: 1, C#-Value: False*

On further research we found that:
When you query a {{TINYINT(1)}} column from MySQL:

* On *Windows*, the MySQL Connector/NET or Pomelo provider may return it as an {{sbyte}} *(numeric)* or {{byte}}.
* On *Linux*, due to *different locale and runtime type-mapping behavior*, it often gets automatically mapped to a {{bool}} when fetched through {{MySqlDataReader}} *?* {{DataTable}}.

This discrepancy arises because:

* MySQL treats {{TINYINT(1)}} as a numeric field ({{0}} or {{1}}).
* But many MySQL .NET providers (like Pomelo) *interpret* {{TINYINT(1)}} *as a* {{BOOLEAN}} by default.
* When {{DataTable.Load(reader)}} runs, it _auto-converts_ the schema based on the providerÆs metadata Ś so {{1 ? true}}, {{0 ? false}}.

The difference between Windows and Linux appears because *different versions of* {{libmysqlclient}} *or* {{MySqlConnector}} may be used internally Ś and their default type mappings differ slightly across environments.

----

For Example:

+MySQL Table+:

CREATE TABLE Users (
  Id INT,
  IsActive TINYINT(1)
);
INSERT INTO Users VALUES (1, 1);

+Stored Procedure+:

CREATE PROCEDURE GetUsers()
BEGIN
  SELECT * FROM Users;
END;

+C# Code+:

using var cmd = new MySqlCommand("GetUsers", connection);
cmd.CommandType = CommandType.StoredProcedure;
var adapter = new MySqlDataAdapter(cmd);
var dt = new DataTable();
adapter.Fill(dt);

+Observed Result+:

On Windows: dt.Rows[0]["IsActive"] ? 1

On Linux: dt.Rows[0]["IsActive"] ? false

This happens because the Linux runtime interprets TINYINT(1) as bool.

So considering this behaviour, we have handled this runtime scenario in linux and now it works for both hosting.

column1: Odoo || SOT API issue
column36:
Hello team,

While trying to onboard the users through Odoo SOT. We are facing API issue.

Same replicated and shown to [~accountid:712020:7d7105fc-4c14-401f-b04b-e8703889b7b0] .
column203: No code change so no impact
column328:
The issue occurred due to changes in the Odoo APIs. Previously, the APIs supported the {{gender}} field in the request/response payload. However, this field is no longer supported.

Because the connector was still sending the {{gender}} field, Odoo returned an unexpected API response. This caused the connector to fail during processing.
column340: The "gender" field has been removed from the v10.10 connector to align with the updated Odoo API specifications. The updated connector has been shared with the client, which resolves the API response mismatch.

column1: PINELABS!!NOT ABLE TO TAKE ACCESS APPWINCP SERVICES
column36:
Hello Team,



I am unable to take App WinSCP Services Access getting ōPreference path errorö
column328:
*Issue:*

# *Datatype Mismatch for* {{sso_service.Lob_Id}}*:*
The {{sso_service.Lob_Id}} field configured on the AGWA server is stored as a *Long Integer (Int64)*. However, the Connector code was designed to handle this field as an *Integer (Int32)*. Due to this mismatch, the system encountered a *memory overflow error* during data processing.
# *Inline Query Compatibility Issue:*
The inline query previously included a {{TOP 1}} condition. Since the database is hosted on *MySQL*, which does not support the {{TOP}} clause, the query resulted in an execution error.

*Resolution:*

# Updated the Connector code to handle {{Lob_Id}} using *Int64 instead of Int32* to ensure compatibility with the database datatype and prevent memory overflow errors.
# As discussed with Param, the {{TOP 1}} *condition has been removed* from the inline query. The *existing* {{WHERE}} *condition already ensures a unique result*, making the {{TOP 1}} clause unnecessary and resolving the MySQL compatibility issue.
column340:
Issue:

Datatype Mismatch for sso_service.Lob_Id:
The sso_service.Lob_Id field configured on the AGWA server is stored as a Long Integer (Int64). However, the Connector code was designed to handle this field as an Integer (Int32). Due to this mismatch, the system encountered a memory overflow error during data processing.

Inline Query Compatibility Issue:
The inline query previously included a TOP 1 condition. Since the database is hosted on MySQL, which does not support the TOP clause, the query resulted in an execution error.

Resolution:

Updated the Connector code to handle Lob_Id using Int64 instead of Int32 to ensure compatibility with the database datatype and prevent memory overflow errors.

As discussed with Param, the TOP 1 condition has been removed from the inline query. The existing WHERE condition already ensures a unique result, making the TOP 1 clause unnecessary and resolving the MySQL compatibility issue.

column1: Log-to-Console ¢ Password Handling Issue | SaaS POC - IFIC client
column36:
h3. *Issue:*

While establishing a session using the *Log to Console* feature, the system requires the *account password* to be identical across both accounts involved in the connection.
If the passwords differ, the system displays a *password error* and prevents session initiation.

h3. *Additional Observations:*

# *SSH access to Linux DB server*
#* Tested by accessing the database hosted on a Linux server via *SSH session*.
#* The issue persists Ś differing passwords result in authentication failure.
# *Sudo / User Switching on Ubuntu 22.04*
#* When using the *sudo su* capability to switch between accounts without password re-entry on Ubuntu 22.04, the same behavior is observed.
#* If the two account passwords are not identical, the Log-to-Console session fails.

h3. *Expected Behavior:*

The *Log to Console* functionality should successfully authenticate as long as each provided password is valid for its respective account.
Password values *should not be required to match* between accounts.

h3. *Impact:*

This limitation creates operational inconvenience for administrators and disrupts customer-facing engagements such as *demonstrations and POCs*.

h3. *Requested Action:*

Kindly review, evaluate, and include a fix for this issue in the upcoming product release.

column1: ESAF | Connectorapi service Failed to write to log file message
column36:
The {{connectorapi.service}} is running successfully, but the application repeatedly fails to write to its log file. Systemd logs show recurring errors indicating that a required value for logging is empty. This suggests a missing or misconfigured logging parameter (e.g., log file path, filename, or logging configuration value).

Despite the service being active, logging failures may hide critical runtime issues and impact troubleshooting.


!image-20260205-055335.png|width=667,alt="image-20260205-055335.png"!
column328:
The ConnectorAPI:Separate Logger Path key in Commonconfig.json for had been set to an non existing path cause path not found exception to be printed in logs.



Issue was resolved the path was correctly updated
column340: Update the ConnectorAPI:Separate Logger Path key in commonconfig.json to an existent and accessible path or set it to empty string.

*Issue:* Unable to perform reconciliation on Salesforce connectorĀ

*_Root Cause_*Ā

* Authorization of API failure due to hardcoded URL in the connector.Ā

* Connector lacked flexibility to dynamically accept endpoint configuration.Ā

*_Analysis_*Ā

* The connector downloaded from the central repository (myVault) was having URL hardcoded.Ā

* Absence of configurable parameters resulted in authorization failures.Ā

*_Resolution / Workaround_*Ā

* Updated connector provided.Ā

column328:
We are getting this error after update *2026-01 Security Update (KB5074109) (26200.7623)*



After applying the latest Windows security patch, the SSO functionality became restricted. As a result, automation and inspection tools are no longer able to retrieve required element identifiers, impacting existing workflows.

h3. Impact

* SSO functionality is not working as expected
* Inspect Tool and AutoIt are unable to fetch UI element IDs
* Automation-dependent processes are blocked
* Potential delay in dependent business operations

h3. 

The newly applied Windows security patch introduced enhanced security restrictions. These restrictions limit access to application UI elements, preventing tools such as Inspect Tool and AutoIt from detecting or reading element IDs required for SSO interaction.

02-06-2026 3:39 PM;712020:a9190ab0-898e-433f-850e-1af35386d015;[~accountid:5d133892a232e50bd6f277cf] Please find the observation below shared by the customer after testing the shared thick client.

*Customer testing Observation :-*

We have validated the following scenarios across laptop and desktop systems to assess the impact of KB5074109 on auto login functionality on ARCON CI SSO :

1.On a laptop using the new Thick Client version 7.3.3.3, service access was successful when KB5074109 was not installed.

2.Accessing the same service through a browser on the laptop was also successful when KB5074109 was not installed.

3.After installing KB5074109 on the same laptop, both the Thick Client (version 7.3.3.3) and browser based access failed to auto-login and remained stuck at the credential prompt.

4.The same behavior was observed on office desktops where KB5074109 is installedŚauto login does not proceed and is blocked at the credential window.

In the CCIL environment, we are using ĀPAM version U16 SP2 (Build 35.8.24 ) for access to critical servers. On these systems, auto login is functioning as expected even when KB5074109 is installed.

Based on the above analysis, the issue appears to be specific to the affected service/client combination after the installation of KB5074109. The same behavior is not observed on the PAM U16 SP2 platform; however, it is reproducible on the ARCON CI SSO version.

02-09-2026 5:28 PM;712020:3e3ca223-2fd1-4f7f-98d1-19c7302275dc;Hello team,
The utility I shared earlier works correctly only when the target server does not have NLA enabled.

When NLA is enabled, authentication happens at the network level, even before the FreeRDP password prompt is displayed. Because of this behavior, our utility fails at this stage. I verified this manually as well, and the behavior is the same.

To resolve this issue, I am currently trying to integrate and use the FreeRDP source code directly and pass the encrypted password via arguments. This work is still in progress as part of the FreeRDP integration.

I will provide an update once I find a working solution.

column409:
03-05-2026 2:26 PM;5d133892a232e50bd6f277cf;[~accountid:5e11f1c7b783d60db0a01cc0] [~accountid:712020:a9190ab0-898e-433f-850e-1af35386d015]  ģ We have successfully resolved this issue today for *Windows RDP* by utilizing *FreeRDP*. However, the same approach cannot be applied to *X-RDP* environments.

Following the *January 2026 Security Update Ś KB5074109 (Build 26200.7623)*, the credential prompt is now displayed within an *isolated secure environment*. Due to this security enhancement, programmatic interaction with the credential pop-up is restricted.

As a result, the prompt can only be interacted with either through application under *administrative privileges* or via a *physical keyboard*, which prevents automated credential injection in the X-RDP scenario.

column1: Unity Bank V10 || AD user creation failing with ōObject already existsö error though user does not present in AD
column36:
Hello Team, we have received a request from SOT for AD user creation. However, multiple user IDs are failing during the provisioning process.

The lifecycle logs show the error *ōObject already existsö*, but upon verification, no such user object exists in Active Directory.

This discrepancy is preventing successful user creation and needs further investigation to identify why the system is detecting an existing object when none is present in AD.
column328: It is technically not possible to have multiple CNs with the same name within the same AD. This is a limitation at the AD level itself, and unfortunately, there is nothing that can be handled or overridden from the CI side in this scenario.
column340: It is technically not possible to have multiple CNs with the same name within the same AD. This is a limitation at the AD level itself, and unfortunately, there is nothing that can be handled or overridden from the CI side in this scenario.
column389: None, Independent of code
column401:
02-09-2026 4:49 PM;712020:e94b6f5f-bd51-478c-aad4-ef6a069b850c;Had a call with [~accountid:712020:7cb1f26c-6e7e-4b1e-82a0-1bc82e93a589]  to review the *ōObject already existsö* issue observed in Production for a few users.

We confirmed that these users do *not* currently exist in AD, yet the provisioning still fails with the same error. The same connector works perfectly in UAT, which indicates the connector logic itself is functioning correctly.

We are now investigating why this behavior is occurring only in Production and only for specific users.

One hypothesis discussed was whether a previously existing user (for example, a user with ID 12) might have been disabled or deleted in AD, making it no longer visible, but still present internally (soft-deleted/tombstoned). In such a case, attempting to create a new user with the same ID could trigger the ōObject already existsö error.

However, Harsh confirmed that user IDs are always unique and are not reused, which reduces the likelihood of this scenario.

Further analysis is required on the Production AD state  to identify the exact cause.

[~accountid:5e17c137800be30d9fba9377] FYI.

[~accountid:712020:15015ff8-f339-4c97-880d-1608459912ef] keeping you in loop too just in case intervention of your team is required.

column1: Xceedance | CI-PAM | Intermittent Linux SSO Connectivity Issue
column36:
Hi Team,

This is to inform you that we have recently noticed an intermittent issue with Linux SSO connectivity.

Based on our observations, we are sometimes able to connect to Linux servers successfully; however, at other times the SSO does not work. In such cases, the connection status shows as ōConnecting,ö after which the ARCOS popup closes and no further action occurs.

We also checked the Task Manager and observed that *arcosputty* is not launching whenever the SSO connection to the Linux server fails.

This behavior is observed primarily with Linux servers. Other services such as AWB and RDP are working smoothly without any issues.

CI Version: 10.10.007 V2 Windows.

column1: Unity Bank V10 || AD Connector incorrectly mapping CN (Full Name) and updating EmployeeID on target AD
column36:
Hello Team, 

We are experiencing an issue in the *AD Connector attribute mapping* where:

* *CN* is correctly mapped to *Full Name* in the configuration.
* In the *DI section*, the value appears correctly as *Full Name*.
* However, during provisioning to *Target AD*, instead of updating *CN/Full Name*, it is *unexpectedly updating the EmployeeID attribute*.
column328:
RCA: There was knowledge gap in the mapping in the connector, at place of CN (canonical name) was  mapped with username. Due to which 10-15 users go affected with wrong value.

Action: Done the required changes in LDAP bot to modify the CN name for reconciliation

column1: Login/Logout time and Timeline not displayed in Video Logs for QA ANB MS sessions
column36:
In the *QA ANB MS environment*, the *Video Log page* does not display the *login time, logout time, and session timeline* for both active and inactive sessions.

Due to the missing timeline and timestamps, it becomes difficult to track session duration and user activity accurately during audit and review.

!Timeline issue.mp4|width=320,alt="Timeline issue.mp4"!

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Login to the application.
# Start a session in the *QA ANB MS environment*.
# Perform some activity and then *log out* of the session.
# Navigate to *Session Monitoring ? Video Logs*.
# Open the video log details for:
#* An *active session*
#* An *inactive session*

----

h3. *Actual Result*

* *Login time and logout time* are not displayed on the Video Log page.
* *Timeline* is not visible for both active and inactive sessions.

column328: Configuration issue : The {{New Timer Update Mechanism - Is Enabled}} flag was disabled. The {{New Timer Update Mechanism - Is Enabled}} flag in settings module must be kept enabled for viewing session timelines in session monitoring module.
column340: The New Timer Update Mechanism - Is Enabled flag in settings module was enabled and the functionality was tested to be working.
column375: This ticket is prima facie a Configuration issue as The {{New Timer Update Mechanism - Is Enabled}} flag was disabled. The {{New Timer Update Mechanism - Is Enabled}} flag in settings module must be kept enabled for viewing session timelines in session monitoring module.
column389:
1. Login To CI.
2. Enable the The New Timer Update Mechanism - Is Enabled  flag in settings module.
3. Take SSO.
4. Close SSO session.
5. Go to Session Monitoring -> View Session Video Log Details.

column1: Unity Bank V10 || Digital OneFin error occurs during Full Reconciliation
column204:
There are no code review comments.

There were changes in the connector.json file.
column325: No Environment
column328:
The issue occurred because the entitlements provided by the user master API were in integer format. However, our ReconDI API expects entitlements to be passed as strings. This mismatch in data type caused the API to fail for some users.

Looking to the above behaviour from the clientÆs application we have made changes in the connector.json file to incorporate this behaviour. We have provided an updated connector to the implementation team.
column340:
The issue occurred because the entitlements provided by the user master API were in integer format. However, our ReconDI API expects entitlements to be passed as strings. This mismatch in data type caused the API to fail for some users.

Looking to the above behaviour from the clientÆs application we have made changes in the connector.json file to incorporate this behaviour. We have provided an updated connector to the implementation team.
column375:
*Root Cause:*
The entitlements provided by the user master API were in integer format. The ReconDI API, however, expected entitlements to be passed as strings. This mismatch in data type caused the API to fail for users with groups.

*Investigation:*

* Initially, the connector was functioning correctly in both UAT and production environments.
* The client confirmed there were no changes to the APIs.
* After further investigation using logs, DIs, and APIs in both environments, it was observed that users without groups were processed successfully, while users with groups encountered failures.

*Resolution:*

* We modified the connector to include a data type conversion for entitlements.
* Specifically, we ensured that entitlements received from the user master API are converted from integers to strings before being passed to the ReconDI API.
* With this fix, the entitlements now work as expected for all users, regardless of whether they have groups.

01-13-2026 8:34 PM;712020:7d7105fc-4c14-401f-b04b-e8703889b7b0;The issue was identified as a data type mismatch, where entitlements were being sent as integers from the user master API, but the ReconDI API required them to be in string format. To address this, we updated the connector to convert the entitlements to strings before sending them to the API.

Additionally, we added a decision step for groups in the provisioning process to ensure more flexible handling of group-related entitlements. This ensures that entitlements now work for all users without issues.

The connector has been tested and is working.

Ā

column1: CBOB || App IBM Access Client Solutions not working
column36:
Hello Team, 

We have onboarded infra asset App IBM Access Client Solutions on client side. However, when taking the connection from CI PAM we are getting an error  *Error occurred while connector launch*.

!image-20260108-043942.png|width=437,alt="image-20260108-043942.png"!



We have checked the following:
correct preference path is configured.
Client is able to launch the application without PAM successfully.
Port is allowed from gateway server.
Also tried to onboard the application as IBM AS400 but receiving same error.

Connectorlog is attached to the ticket.

Please assist on this.
column328:
*In PAM:*
There are two settings to provide the path for the AS400 exe and the .kpm file, represented by spm_id- 84 and 154. The UI allows configuring the preference path for both of these fields.

*In CI:*
There are also two settings for providing the path for the AS400 exe and the .kpm file, represented by spm_id 84 and 154. However, the UI provides only one option to set the preference path.
As a result, when the preference path is set from the UI, it updates the path for the first spm_id-84 based on the default ascending order.
However, the connector uses spm_id-154 to launch the exe.

Upon reviewing the code, we confirmed that the connector does not use the preference path associated with spm_id -84. 

Therefore, we deleted the spm_id 84 settings using a script so that the workspace now sets the preference path for the default spm_id 154
column340:

column1: CBOB || Windows RDP disconnecting
column36:
Hello Team,


We are facing an issue where a user is not able to access windows RDP through CI PAM. User connects to the RDP session and then randomly session goes in reconnecting state. 

!Screenshot 2025-12-20 034715-20251219-221715.png|width=342,alt="Screenshot 2025-12-20 034715-20251219-221715.png"!

h3. Troubleshooting Steps Performed

* Tested ping & telnet from Gateway ? Target Server
? No packet drop
* Tested ping & telnet from End User ? Gateway
? Stable, no drop
* Bypassed PAM and accessed servers via direct RDP
? No disconnections
* Issue reproduced for other servers from user machine via PAM.
* Verified other users can access the same servers via PAM without disconnections
* Checked Windows Event Viewer logs on user machine
? No relevant errors
* No logs getting generated in ACM folder.
* Added ARCON plugin folder to Symantec AV exception list
* Restarted user machine
* Uninstalled and reinstalled ARCON plugin
* Verified user internet connection

Please assist on this.
column328:
Following are the details :

*Issue*: This issue was a dual monitor issue in which when the user was dragging the RDP to another monitor it was getting disconnected.

*Solution*: This use case was handled in the code and verified in the latest build (10.11.000). The code changes were in the ARCOSRDPTerminal. So for this particular ticket, we back merged the changes in the same version (10.10.007_HF) that the customer is ON and we had supplied a patch to the implementation team.

So, the RCA for this ticket has been marked as ōResolved in Latest Versionö
column340: This issue has been solved in the latest version of CI (v10.10.008 aka v10.11.000). So we had to code the same into 10.10.007_HF the version that the customer is ON and share a patch with implementation team.

column1: Connector | Access Control treats literal commands as restricted; ōOneSided Clipboardö triggers error on Linux when entered via terminal
column36:
*Description:*
When Access Control policies are applied, the system is blocking commands based on literal matches rather than actual execution behavior. Specifically, attempting to type and execute the ōOneSided Clipboardö command on Linux results in an error, as the policy considers the string itself a restricted command.

Same behaviour is replicated for Other Restrictions such as AllowClipboard, AllowClipboardAGW, etc.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Ensure Access Control Profile Created for OneSideClipboard
# Take SSO of LINUX
# Copy paste the ōOneSideClipBoardö

column328:
The commands that are listed as a restricted commands, Putty restricts them. "Other Restricted" type of commands were never discussed during the requirement gathering so we missed to implement this in the putty.
Now we have implemented in the Putty that any command listed in the restricted command list but at the same time the type is "Other Restricted" we simply allow them to execute.

These changes are done in the putty.exe (C Language) which takes care of this condition.

column328:
+As per the troubleshoot we observed below behaviour with the customerÆs application:+

Users were deprovisioned because the *Modify Entitlement* operation was failing when the asset group had *no roles assigned*. During investigation with ArulÆs team, it was confirmed that modifying entitlements without any associated roles is not supported, which caused the failure.

*Resolution:*

This application behaviour has been noticed and observed along with implementation team. So, the asset in *UAT* has been placed under *maintenance mode* while changes are made, ensuring no admin email notifications are triggered during updates.

So in a nutshell there are no code changes required here and it has been a knowledge gap for the understanding of the customerÆs application (out of arconÆs purview).

column1: SSO | RHEL client session uses Target Machine camera/mic instead of Local Machine camera/mic post?SSO, despite Access Control enabling RedirectCamera and RedirectMic
column36:
*Description:*
When connecting from a *RHEL client* via *SSO* to the *Target Machine*, the session incorrectly accesses the *Target MachineÆs* camera and microphone. The *local* camera/mic *are not redirected* even though the *Access Control profile* explicitly enables *RedirectCamera* and *RedirectMic*

*Expected Result:*
After SSO login, the session should use *Local Machine* camera and microphone via redirection (apps should see and use local devices).

*Actual Result:*
Session uses the *Target MachineÆs* camera/mic; local devices are *not* available for selection. No redirection state/icon appears for camera/mic.

*Reproduction Steps:*

# On *RHEL* _(version)_ client, ensure Access Control profile has *Other ? RedirectCamera + RedirectMic* enabled.
# Connect to *Target Machine* via *SSO*
# Test the Mic and Camera in Web.
# Observe: Devices available are *from the Target Machine*, not the local client.
# Repeat the same steps on *QA ANBMs* ? Local devices *are* redirected (works as expected)

Details: -

[https://identityqa-rhel.arconnet.com|https://identityqa-rhel.arconnet.com]

Uname - ARCOSADMIN

Asset - Windows

ALias Name - MicTest

column1: Xceedance | CI-PAM | session lockout time issue
column36:
Hi Team,

We are facing an issue with the service session timeout. This is to inform you that when we create a Windows RDP service without adding the {{<NLA>}} tag and try to access the service, then later add the {{<NLA>}} tag without setting the session lockout time, we encounter an issue. When we use SSO for that service, the session locks instantly and the login window appears.

However, for a second Windows RDP service, if we add the {{<NLA>}} tag before accessing it for the first time, we are able to access the service without any issue.

The global session timeout is set to 300, but we are still facing this problem. Please find the attached screenshots for reference.

CI Version: 10.10.007 V2 Windows

column401:
12-12-2025 1:39 PM;712020:e51661a4-3066-4090-b235-16860d919cdc;After investigating the issue, we identified that the *Idle Session Lockout* setting was not configured. This field is mandatory and must have a value selected in the Advanced section. After configuring the Idle Session Lockout value in both *Digital Asset* and *Digital Identity*, the SSO functionality started working as expected.

[~accountid:712020:e60068c8-e69c-4fd8-bdd5-72f6154f9c36] , could you please confirm the same?

FYI: [~accountid:5e17c137800be30d9fba9377]
column402: 12-17-2025 12:08 PM;712020:e60068c8-e69c-4fd8-bdd5-72f6154f9c36;Hi [~accountid:712020:e51661a4-3066-4090-b235-16860d919cdc] , after adding the session timeout, we are able to access the service. Thanks for your support. However, the question remains: for some services, the session timeout is not set, yet we are still able to access those services without any issues. Is there any reason for this behavior?
column403:
12-18-2025 2:40 PM;712020:e51661a4-3066-4090-b235-16860d919cdc;Thank you [~accountid:712020:e60068c8-e69c-4fd8-bdd5-72f6154f9c36]for the confirmation. Good to know that the issue has been resolved.

Please note that setting the *idle session timeout* is mandatory for every asset. By default, when we create an asset, In Advance section, it iss visible that the idle session timeout dropdown is set to 10 by default. However, in your case, while uploading the asset, the idle session timeout value was not selected. If no value is set, it indicates that the asset configuration is incomplete, and proper functionality of SSO cannot be ensured.

Asset configuration is a critical prerequisite. Once the correct configuration was applied by setting the idle session timeout, the SSO worked without any issues.

I have verified this behavior on *HfArcon* and *ArconMS (DevInt)* environments, where a default value for idle session timeout is present. In your scenario, it appears that *bulk asset creation* may be the reason the dropdown did not retain any value.

As a resolution, you may either manually set the idle session timeout for the affected asset or report this to the Admin Console UI so that a default value is enforced during bulk asset creation.

FYI [~accountid:5e17c137800be30d9fba9377]

column1: SSO through AGW plus & AGWA not working
column36: SSO through AGW is not working. Upon taking SSO of Windows and Linux through AGW plus, an error is thrown, followed by a still screen.
column204:
No Code Review Comments.

Unit Test Case: Not applicable since the change is in the connector.exe
SonarLint: Not applicable.
column325:
1. Verify that the AGW Plus server can be mapped to the asset successfully.- Pass
2. Verify that Windows RDP connection works through AGW Plus without any error.- Pass
3. Verify that SSH Linux connection works through AGW Plus without any error.- Pass
4. Verify that the user is able to perform activities inside the AGW session.- Pass
5. Verify that the video logs for the AGW session are displayed properly in Session Monitoring.- Pass
6. Verify that the AGW Plus server can be unmapped from the asset successfully.- Pass
7. Verify that after un-mapping, the normal (non-AGW) connection works properly without any issues.- Pass
8. Verify that there is no impact on connectivity or activity after un-mapping AGW Plus from the asset.- Pass
column328:
Issue: Datatype mismatch for {{sso_service_log.ats_id}} field .The {{sso_service_log.ats_id}} for the configured AGWPlus server is an long integer. The Connector code is designed to handle a integer for the {{sso_service_log.ats_id}}. Thus we encounter a memory overflow error.

Cause: When the activity to convert primary keys of tables to UUID was undertaken, concerned teams had modified the datatype for {{sso_service_log.ats_id}} to long from int. This caused newly configured AGW servers to receive a long UUID. Since the scenario of configuring a new AGW server and performing SSO had not been tested after the datatype update, the issue went unnoticed. Thus Connector code was not updated to handle this change. Recently when QA had configured a new AGW server and performed a SSO with the same, the issue was discovered.

Resolution: Change type conversions from Int32 to Int64 when handling {{ats_id}} in Connector code.
column340:
When the activity to convert primary keys of tables to UUID was undertaken, concerned teams had modified the datatype for ats_id to long from int. This caused newly configured AGW servers to receive a long UUID. Since the scenario of configuring a new AGW server and performing SSO had not been tested after the datatype update, the issue went unnoticed. Thus Connector code was not updated to handle this change. Recently when QA had configured a new AGW server and performed a SSO with the same, the issue was discovered.
Thus Changed type conversions from Int32 to Int64 when handling ats_id in Connector code.

column405:
01-23-2026 6:45 PM;712020:80aad7c2-5438-485e-91b8-3a83b1333563;Hello [~accountid:712020:d195ba7d-4dd7-4317-a04f-ac40bba355e5] .

As discussed with you in call this appears to be the same issue where the business application is hosted on the same domain as CI. When any application is closed, the ARCON BROWSER EXTENSION (ABE) clears the cache this led to failure to open another business application.

Kindly take this forward and let us know if any additional details are required from our side.
column406:
01-27-2026 4:43 PM;712020:d195ba7d-4dd7-4317-a04f-ac40bba355e5;Hi [~accountid:5ae9b87eae7a832d555b6d5b]  and [~accountid:712020:cc8f6499-0fb1-4f4a-980b-c5498f479323]  , 

As discussed in the last session, I will need to perform  feasibility check for the issue.

According to the Chrome extension documentation, the {{chrome.browsingData}} API clears cookies at the domain level. When cookies are cleared for {{www.example.com}}, they are also cleared for {{*.example.com}} by default. Therefore, this behavior cannot be modified using this API.

!CI-18125.jpg|thumbnail!

There is another API that supports this use caseŚthe {{chrome.cookies}} APIŚbut we need to assess its impact before proceeding. I will share a patch with [~accountid:712020:cc8f6499-0fb1-4f4a-980b-c5498f479323] .

column328:
Issue: SSO fails / unable to launch connectors
Cause Identified: The SSO was failing due to unresponsiveness of webdtcore apis. The WebDTcore module apis were failing due to the absence of Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0) in publish folder on application server. A thorough investigation revealed usage of outdated System.Data.SqlClient dll which invokes Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0). 
Further more , encryption of password is happening with one tenantÆs Key and the decryption of the password is happening with another tenantÆs key. As a result password authentication fails due to incorrect creds and login fails.
Solution implemented: All references of deprecated System.Data.SqlClient dll across modules in arcon_ci_projects repo were updated to the successor Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.22.0). Further , SSE changes for password encryption changes were implemented  to resolve the password authentication issue.

column328:
Issue: SSO fails / unable to launch connectors

+*Troubleshoot 1*+:
Cause Identified: The SSO was failing due to unresponsiveness of webdtcore apis. The WebDTcore module apis were failing due to the absence of Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0) in publish folder on application server. A thorough investigation revealed usage of outdated System.Data.SqlClient dll which invokes Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0). 
Further more , encryption of password is happening with one tenantÆs Key and the decryption of the password is happening with another tenantÆs key. As a result password authentication fails due to incorrect creds and login fails.
Solution implemented: All references of deprecated System.Data.SqlClient dll across modules in arcon_ci_projects repo were updated to the successor Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.22.0). Further , SSE changes for password encryption changes were implemented  to resolve the password authentication issue.



+*Troubleshoot 2*+:

While Troubleshooting we found that when the SSO is launching the webdtcore sends the password to putty ATH and the Putty ATH throws below error.

!image-20251202-113738.png|width=590,alt="image-20251202-113738.png"!

[~accountid:60781ac31d2b25006990bef2]  & [~accountid:712020:9e3cdd10-721f-48ab-8735-98fb875f5c66]  on troubleshooting found that the key for tenant 1 is used for encryption and for decryptiing another tenantÆs key is used due to which this issue is cropping up. On furhter troubleshooting [~accountid:712020:9e3cdd10-721f-48ab-8735-98fb875f5c66]  found that the multi-tenant sceanrio was not handled in the ARCONServerSE.dll. The respective changes have been done and deployed.

column328:
Issue: SSO fails / unable to launch connectors

+*Troubleshoot 1*+:
Cause Identified: The SSO was failing due to unresponsiveness of webdtcore apis. The WebDTcore module apis were failing due to the absence of Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0) in publish folder on application server. A thorough investigation revealed usage of outdated System.Data.SqlClient dll which invokes Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0). 
Further more , encryption of password is happening with one tenantÆs Key and the decryption of the password is happening with another tenantÆs key. As a result password authentication fails due to incorrect creds and login fails.
Solution implemented: All references of deprecated System.Data.SqlClient dll across modules in arcon_ci_projects repo were updated to the successor Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.22.0). Further , SSE changes for password encryption changes were implemented  to resolve the password authentication issue.

+*Troubleshoot 2*+:

While Troubleshooting we found that when the SSO is launching the webdtcore sends the password to putty ATH and the Putty ATH throws below error.

!image-20251202-113738.png|width=590,alt="image-20251202-113738.png"!

[~accountid:60781ac31d2b25006990bef2]  & [~accountid:712020:9e3cdd10-721f-48ab-8735-98fb875f5c66]  on troubleshooting found that the key for tenant 1 is used for encryption and for decryptiing another tenantÆs key is used due to which this issue is cropping up. On furhter troubleshooting [~accountid:712020:9e3cdd10-721f-48ab-8735-98fb875f5c66]  found that the multi-tenant sceanrio was not handled in the ARCONServerSE.dll. The respective changes have been done and deployed.
column340: All references of deprecated System.Data.SqlClient dll across modules in arcon_ci_projects repo were updated to the successor Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll. Further , SSE changes for password encryption changes were implemented  to resolve the password authentication issue.

column328:
Issue: SSO fails / unable to launch connectors
Cause Identified: The SSO was failing due to unresponsiveness of webdtcore apis. The WebDTcore module apis were failing due to the absence of Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0) in publish folder on application server. A thorough investigation revealed usage of outdated System.Data.SqlClient dll which invokes Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.0.0.0). 
Further more , encryption of password is happening with one tenantÆs Key and the decryption of the password is happening with another tenantÆs key. As a result password authentication fails due to incorrect creds and login fails.
Solution implemented: All references of deprecated System.Data.SqlClient dll across modules in arcon_ci_projects repo were updated to the successor Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll (v=5.22.0). Further , SSE changes for password encryption changes were implemented  to resolve the password authentication issue.
column334:
While Troubleshooting we found that when the SSO is launching the webdtcore sends the password to putty ATH and the Putty ATH throws below error.

!image-20251202-113738.png|width=590,alt="image-20251202-113738.png"!

[~accountid:60781ac31d2b25006990bef2]  & [~accountid:712020:9e3cdd10-721f-48ab-8735-98fb875f5c66]  on troubleshooting found that the key for tenant 1 is used for encryption and for decryptiing another tenantÆs key is used due to which this issue is cropping up. On furhter troubleshooting [~accountid:712020:9e3cdd10-721f-48ab-8735-98fb875f5c66]  found that the multi-tenant sceanrio was not handled in the ARCONServerSE.dll. The respective changes have been done and deployed.
column340: All references of deprecated System.Data.SqlClient dll across modules in arcon_ci_projects repo were updated to the successor Microsoft.Data.SqlClient dll. Further , SSE changes for password encryption changes were implemented  to resolve the password authentication issue.

The following error occurs during step 2 (APICall):

An error occurred during step 2 - 'APICall' Error message: {"error":{"code":"Request_BadRequest","message":"Invalid value specified for property 'employeeId' of resource 'User'.","details":[{"code":"InvalidLength","message":"The employeeId should be between 1 and 16 characters.","target":"employeeId"}],"innerError":{"date":"2025-10-31T10:46:06","request-id":"211642be-f550-41a6-8871-36549ddfd534","client-request-id":"211642be-f550-41a6-8871-36549ddfd534"}}}
column325: NA
column328:
Based on the debugging performed during our call, the connector currently used in the environment is expecting the *employeeId* attribute while creating a user. However, since the connector was recently changed from the database, the text fields displayed on the UI still correspond to the old connector. As a result, the *employeeId* value cannot be passed through the UI.

Summary:

# The old connector was already uploaded.
# Separately a database query was run by implementation which had the new connector.
# Due to the scripts the formbuilder (the UI) & the fields in the database were a mis-match where the EmployeeID was missing. Due to this reason there was this issue.

column1: |RBAC| HTTP Error 403 encountered when connecting to SSH Linux asset via Workspace
column36:
After assigning the *Workspace* privilege to a user, the user is able to log in successfully, and in the *Control Panel*, only the Workspace module is visible as expected.

However, when the user attempts to establish a connection to an *SSH Linux asset*, an error message appears:

{quote}*ōWeb method failure, response code ¢ 403ö*{quote}

This indicates that the user does not have sufficient permission or the required API access for performing the connection operation, even though the Workspace privilege has been granted.

column328:
While trying to troubleshoot the issue was working fine. We are not able to reproduce the issue.

Logged in user is not having privilege for Access control hence Accesscontrol APi endpoint is not authorizibg.
column334:
After the connector is launched from the workspace, in the due process of setting up the {{ArcosPutty.exe}} , the exe is consuming an api of the Access Control module : {{GetMappedCommandsForConnectorSessionTimebased}}. The mentioned api is returning 403 - forbidden error. The further functionality of SSO breaks as a result. Observation on further investigation reveals that following the RBAC changes , all endpoints of Access Control Module require necessary privileges assigned to the requesting user. Here the concerned user only had privileges for Workspace.

 Thus, the access control endpoints (namely {{GetMappedCommandsForConnectorSessionTimebased}} , {{GetMappedProcessesForConnectorSession}}, {{GetMappedCommandsForConnectorSession}}) being utilized by the connectors need to have necessary privileges to Access Control module for the SSO functionality to accomplish successfully.

column1: Unity Bank V10 || Date of Joining format mismatch between SOT and Oracle Fusion
column36: Hello Team, The _Date of Joining_ field received from SOT is in *DD-MM-YYYY* format, but Oracle Fusion requires the date in *YYYY-MM-DD* format. Due to this mismatch, the JML process is failing.
column204:
There has been no code change for this ticket

Unit Test Case: Not Applicable
SonarLint: Not applicable
column328:
*Issue:*
Date of Joining (DOJ) format mismatch between SOT and Oracle Fusion. The expected date format was *YYYY-MM-DD*.

*Summary:*
A mismatch was reported in the DOJ between the SOT file and Oracle Fusion for Unity Bank V10. Upon verification with Harsh, it was confirmed that the DOJ present in the system was correct and there was no actual issue with the data. However, although the ōProvisioning was successö status was visible, the user record was not appearing in the full reconciliation logs.

*Root Cause:*
The required roles were not assigned to the specific user by Harsh during provisioning. Due to these missing roles, the userÆs record did not appear in the user list during full reconciliation, leading to a misunderstanding that there was a DOJ mismatch.

*Resolution:*
After assigning the appropriate roles to the user, the record became visible in the full reconciliation logs. The provisioning success status was correctly displayed, and the DOJ matched as expected between SOT and Oracle Fusion.

*Status:*
? Issue resolved

*Action Taken:*
Roles assigned to user ? Full reconciliation re-run ? Verified successful provisioning and DOJ alignment.

+*_NOTE: NO ISSUE IN CONNECTOR_*+

*Owner:*
Harsh

column328:
When both the SSM & Image Capture are enabled, both the features call respective apis. Now in this process there are two token requests which gets generated. So, the one who got the first token tries to access the subsequent api but now the first token has become invalid or obsolete. So, when image capture uses the first token, it shows "Token Unauthorized" and the session closes before the session extension popup shows up.

Ā

This issue has been resolved but during the time it was fixed the branch Dev_35.8.29 branch for repository PAM was locked and couldnt be unlocked. So, since we have fixed this earlier, we have moved the code into the branch 35.8.29_HotFix for the PAM to resolve this issue.

* Logged into the database and Redis.
* *Updated encrypted values* in all databases:
** Replaced encrypted *Public IP* with encrypted *Private IP*.
* After the correction, *assets became accessible via AGW (Advanced)*.

column1: Windows & Linux Connector Not Accessible in 10.10.007 V2
column36:
Hi Team,
We have deployment 10.10.007 V2 and we have faced issue while accessing the windows & linux connectors.As per troubleshooting we came to know that 445 id in sso_arcos_config is passing the null value.Please find the logs attached.
column328:
Hello, below is the update -





	1. The connectors were not accessible because the video-recorder was killing the connector exe, this issue is fixed. RCA - The video-recorder exe reads the global setting and caches it. We get a list of records that hold the setting info like setting id, default value, max value, etc from the sso_arcos_config table. Locally it is cached by a key value mechanism where the key is the id of the setting and value is the defaut value of the setting, there was a setting whose default value was NULL, and adding a null value in cache throws an error that value cannot be null, because of which the videorecorder exe closed all the sessions, A null checker was added to handle this. 

	2. SSH Linux Connections gets closed - We can visibly see that putty gets launched and gets terminated later without any error message. Outside PAM also the same behaviour is observed, hence it is not an issue with PAM.
column401:

# The connectors were not accessible because the video-recorder was killing the connector exe, this issue is fixed. RCA - The video-recorder exe reads the global setting and caches it. We get a list of records that hold the setting info like setting id, default value, max value, etc from the sso_arcos_config table. Locally it is cached by a key value mechanism where the key is the id of the setting and value is the defaut value of the setting, there was a setting whose default value was NULL, and adding a null value in cache throws an error that value cannot be null, because of which the videorecorder exe closed all the sessions, A null checker was added to handle this.

column403:
11-25-2025 11:17 AM;712020:2540bcd5-8b3b-48f2-903b-cbcc772aee0d;*Issue Summary:* Images/Video logs were not getting generated for any service accessed in CI PAM. The issue affected all session recordings despite video logging being enabled.

Observations & Initial Checks:

*Video Log Setting for Connector:*

Verified that the Video Log option was enabled for the connector in Admin Settings.

*Asset-Level Video Logging:*

Confirmed that the Video Log option was enabled for the specific asset involved.

*Log Folder Configuration:*

Checked that the log folder path configured in Admin Settings was correct and accessible.

*ACM Logs:*

Found repeated ōinvalid parameterö errors in the ACM logs in Plugin folder during session.

*Root Cause:*

The setting Network Bandwidth Dependent Adaptive Image Quality was causing image generation to fail.

*Resolution:*

As per the developerÆs recommendation, we disabled the setting ōNetwork Bandwidth Dependent Adaptive Image Quality.ö After disabling this option, session images started generating successfully for all sessions.

column36:
We are facing an issue while onboarding the Oracle SAP connector in the CI IAM internal environment.
Please find the attached screenshots showing the onboarding query, the error message, as well as the ToD database query and table details for reference.

The issue occurs during the connector onboarding process, and it prevents successful integration with the Oracle SAP system.

* Error message screenshot
* ToD database query and table screenshot

column325: This ticket was received for QA validation in Build 10.10.008 (Build 8). As there is currently no internal environment for SAP Connector. Hence, the ticket status is being updated to æQA PassedÆ for client release.
column328:
Initially, the issue was caused because the *database connection was not being established properly*, leading to failures when executing the query during the Oracle SAP connector onboarding process. This was fixed by correcting the *DB connectivity configuration* in the *new production environment*, which resolved the original issue. (as below):
1. Connector Side: There was an old database connection url that we were using which was provided in the initial integration with the customer. Now since the database connection url has been changed we updated the same and it worked. (done by connector & onboarding team)

# Configuration Issue: There was a configuration issue which was resolved by onboarding team.

However, the current new ticket [*CI-16673*|https://arcon-tech-solution.atlassian.net/browse/CI-16673] is *not related to the earlier DB connectivity issue*. It is a *new change request* raised due to *add more SOT fields* in Step 2 of the Oracle SAP connector setup in the client environment.

The connector JSON files shared earlier were only meant for the *version upgrade*, and *not for new SOT fields to be added* .

So, this ticket is covered and a new sub-task has been created to cater to the change request.

column1: CLONE - Command logs not displaying in Session Monitoring for RHEL environment
column36: In the RHEL environment, the connection is working fine, and all activities can be performed successfully. However, after closing the session and checking in *Session Monitoring*, the *command logs are not displaying*, even though the *video logs are visible accurately*.
column74:
Troubleshot the raised issue . Updated RCA :

The CommandSessionLogNew API process flow in WebDTCore has been modified according to [CI-15181] POC - Command Log via Kafka and Insert into DB and OpenSearch - Jira. Logging is now being handled by producing topics to Kafka, instead of using the previously existing stored procedure calls.
 The functionality is failing on REHL as the consumer service is not deployed on REHL env for the concerned topic.

column1: Unable to launch any Business Asset.
column36:
While trying to launch the Business Assets, the connection fails.

* In the API response, the message is showing *ōconnection parameterö*.
* In the ARCOS logs, the following error message is observed: *ōLength cannot be less than zero.ö*

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Attempt to launch any Business Asset.
# Observe the API response and ARCOS logs.

*Expected Result:*
The Business Asset should launch successfully without any errors.
column204:
There are no code review comments.

The API endpoint  /ARCOSWebAPI/ARCOSWebDT2.asmx/ReturnOptimizedDataSet  is returning incorrect value..
LinqDataFetching flag in commonconfig had been set to true on deployed environments.
Thus, the incorrect data is being fetched via LINQs instead of SPs.
The API is responding with correct data after flag is set to false.

column1: Unity Small Finance Bank|| CI5.3.9||Issue with Missing Additional Roles Post-Reconciliation
column36:
*Dear Team,*

We are currently facing an issue where *additional roles are not visible after reconciliation*. However, these roles are present in the *API response*.

This suggests that while the roles are correctly fetched via API, they are either not being processed correctly during reconciliation or not being displayed in the UI.

!image (1).jfif|width=800,height=561,alt="image (1).jfif"!

!image.jfif|width=800,height=545,alt="image.jfif"!

!image (2).jfif|width=800,height=525,alt="image (2).jfif"!
column204: There are no code review comments. There was a change in the json file.
column328:
* *Issue:* Additional roles appear in API response but not post-reconciliation in UI, indicating processing/display problem.
* *Root cause:* Missing pagination in the Get Roles API caused incomplete role retrieval in the connector.

As discussed with the client, pagination was added to the *Get Roles API.* Previously, pagination was not included in the API, which is why it was not part of the connector and roles were not appearing.
As agreed during the call, pagination will be incorporated into the connector for CI version *V10.10* to address this issue.
column340: Pagination will be added to the connector in CI version V10.10 to fix the issue.
column375:

* *Resolution:* Pagination will be added to the connector in CI version V10.10 to fix the issue.

column1: Connector Issue - Putty auto password enter
column36:
Putty has been logged in with user arcon

if Password for logged in user is pormpt then the putty connector should enter the password 

but if Password for any other user is prompt then putty connector should wait for user input.

!image-20250828-091459.png|width=563,height=79,alt="image-20250828-091459.png"!
column328: The Linux operating system typically anticipates the password of the user who is currently logged in whenever we execute any command that begins with sudo. However, in this case, it asks for the password of the user on the other machine, and how will PuTTY determine if it is asking for the password of the user on the other machine or the currently logged-in user? Prinstan therefore suggested a solution, using a pop-up to ask the user if they would like to enter their password automatically. If they do, PuTTY will enter their current userÆs password automatically; if not, they will be able to enter it manually.
column401:
09-03-2025 6:19 PM;63490f852edf195c40ab72d8;Hi,

As suggested by ?Prinstan, the modifications were made, and the binary was made available for testing.

Thanks

column1: Connector Issue - Putty
column36:
Putty issue popupģ

Information should be like 

ōPlease wait while we update the command logs as requested by Audit.(Max wait time 60 seconds)ö  



!image-20250828-090527.png|width=645,height=681,alt="image-20250828-090527.png"!
column328: There is no problem with this. It was implemented this way only. If the user presses a key while we execute a command that requires a password to be automatically entered, we display this popup and erase the user's keyboard input for that moment. We put this popup in place because if we let the user type anything on the keyboard, the password will be visible on the terminal.

column1: Airtel B2B | Passwords visible in command logs
column36:
Hello Team,
I would like to report an issue where passwords are visible in command logs in Session monitoring
column204:
None.
Unit Test Case: There are no unit test case for this project. So, no unit test case to review.
SonarLint: There are no sonarlint errors found.
column325:
1. Validate that when a Linux DI asset is configured with a 1-minute lockout time, the user is able to re-enter the password after lockout and continue executing commands successfully without any disruption. - Pass.
2. Validate that when the user copy-pastes the password after the Linux SSO session locks, the system still allows execution of commands without failure.  - Pass.
3. Validate that in session monitoring, for the same session ID, the entered password is not displayed in the command logs even though commands are executed successfully.  - Pass.
4. Validate that the same behavior is maintained when tested on different Linux DI assets and with different lockout durations to ensure uniformity of functionality across configurations. - Pass.
column328: When "session lockout time" screen display and user enter password manually or copy and past , useractivity capture the password also Now we stop capture useractivity on the time of "session lockout time" screen appear and after successful login started again capture.
column340: Code Change has been done we used "proc.ProcessStreamingStatus == StreamingStatus.STREAMING" condition for stop useractivity capture the password. When "session lockout time" screen display and user enter password manually or copy and past , useractivity capture the password also Now we stop capture useractivity on the time of "session lockout time" screen appear and after successful login started again capture.
column389:

3. When session lockout time screen display enter manually or copy past password and login again.
4.Passwords are not visible in command logs in Session monitoring ,this should work as expected

column1: Deutsche Telekom || DI Creation and Reconciliation Failure in M365 Production Environment
column36:
Dear Team,

The M365 DI creation initially failed because the client ID and secret had expired. After updating these credentials, the reconciliation process still failed, and the DI was not created.

Further checks in M365 orchestration logs showed that, for other users, the initial failures were resolved after selecting *"Try Again"*, and provisioning was successful. However, for one specific user, the asset mapping to M365 has not occurred, and the DI remains uncreated.

We request your prompt assistance in identifying the cause and resolving this issue in the production environment.

*RCA*: this was kind of enhancement, that if any of the step fails after provisioning target (around 6-7 steps are there)- in target record is created. but any further step failed. due to which call back doesnÆt occur, thatÆs why it is still in unverified.

*Fix*: if records exists in target and the same record is in unverified state in CI then while reconciliation, we move it to verified.

column1: Web Driver Not Auto-Downloading for App Web Browser in Airtel Environment
column36:
After enabling the *Auto Download Web Driver* option from NG settings, the web driver does *not download automatically* when attempting to connect using {{<CUC>}} and {{<CUE>}} tags. This results in an *error message* during App Web Browser connection.

URL- [https://airtel.arconnet.com/|https://airtel.arconnet.com/]

!b2349d59-b3f9-4c34-9a8f-658d2cd85d9a.jpg|width=800,height=394,alt="b2349d59-b3f9-4c34-9a8f-658d2cd85d9a.jpg"!

!image-20250728-140904.png|width=1359,height=667,alt="image-20250728-140904.png"!

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Go to NG settings and enable the "Auto Download Web Driver" option.
# Attempt to connect using {{<CUC>}} and {{<CUE>}} tags for an App Web Browser connection.
column204:
There is no code change required for this ticket. The fresh deployment from 35.8.29 was done today (24th Sept 2025) and this solved the issue. It was basically due to the old following two dlls:
Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces.dll
WebDriverManager.dll

So there are no code changes,

Unit Test Cases: Not Applicable
SonarLint: Not Applicable

Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces.dll
WebDriverManager.dll

And the new dlls are present in the 35.8.29 branch.

This 35.8.29 branch of PAM repo has been deployed yesterday and due to this the issue has been resolved as shown in the comments.

column1: Video Logs Capture Background Activity When Minimized and Expose Password on Session Lockout
column36:
When an SSO session is initiated and then minimized, minor background activities are still captured in the video logs. Additionally, the user's password is exposed in the log details. This issue also occurs if the session gets locked during this timeŚpasswords remain visible in the log data.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Launch an SSO session.
# Minimize the session or allow it to lock automatically.
# Review the video logs and session log details.

column328: On troubleshooting the IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin api endpoint, we discovered that the primary issue was pertaining to the datatype of primary key of sso_arcos_domains table. The primary key for the sso_arcos_domains table had been updated from int to bigint. However consequential changes in the IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin webdtcore api to handle data overflow cases were absent. As a result, for newly added domains, the sas_id generated was of datatype long , while the api was handling the field as integer causing out of bounds error.
column340:
On troubleshooting the IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin api endpoint, we discovered that the primary issue was pertaining to the datatype of primary key of sso_arcos_domains table. The primary key for the sso_arcos_domains table had been updated from int to bigint. However consequential changes in the IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin webdtcore api to handle data overflow cases were absent. As a result, for newly added domains, the sas_id generated was of datatype long , while the api was handling the field as integer causing out of bounds error.

The following changes were made 

1. variable for sad id was converted from int to long in ArcosCommandSelectParameter model.

2. Changed GetInt32Value to GetInt64Value while assign sad_id value for ArcosCommandSelectParameter object.

Additionally, we discovered that while calling the SP usp_CreateUpdateNewUser, the password is sent in encrypted format. However, the encryption method used (ARC_CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText_XWD()) was throwing exception. The concerned SP stores the password in ssu_password field in sso_users table. We had previously confirmed from the password vault team that ssu_password field should store values encrypted using CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText() method.
The following changes were made 

1. Change of encryption method from  ARC_CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText_XWD() to CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText() for calling the SP usp_CreateUpdateNewUser

07-24-2025 6:17 PM;712020:08760b73-b2e9-4bf2-9d94-0f4ca33c2362;PFA the RCA for the faced issue:
 
On troubleshooting the {{IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin}} api endpoint, we discovered that the primary issue was pertaining to the datatype of primary key of {{sso_arcos_domains}} table. The primary key for the {{sso_arcos_domains}} table had been updated from int to bigint. However consequential changes in the {{IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin}} webdtcore api to handle data overflow cases were absent. As a result, for newly added domains, the sas_id generated was of datatype long , while the api was handling the field as integer causing out of bounds error.

The following changes were made 

# variable for sad id was converted from int to long in {{ArcosCommandSelectParameter}} model.
# Changed GetInt32Value to GetInt64Value while assign {{sad_id}} value for {{ArcosCommandSelectParameter}} object.

Additionally, we discovered that while calling the SP {{usp_CreateUpdateNewUser}}, the password is sent in encrypted format. However, the encryption method used ({{ARC_CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText_XWD()}}) was throwing exception. The concerned SP stores the password in {{ssu_password}} field in {{sso_users}} table. We had previously confirmed from the password vault team that {{ssu_password}} field should store values encrypted using {{CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText()}} method.
The following changes were made 

# Change of encryption method from  {{ARC_CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText_XWD()}} to {{CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText()}} for calling the SP {{usp_CreateUpdateNewUser}}

# As per best practice for recording the video.

column406:
07-24-2025 6:19 PM;712020:08760b73-b2e9-4bf2-9d94-0f4ca33c2362;PFA the RCA for the faced issue:
 
On troubleshooting the {{IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin}} api endpoint, we discovered that the primary issue was pertaining to the datatype of primary key of {{sso_arcos_domains}} table. The primary key for the {{sso_arcos_domains}} table had been updated from int to bigint. However consequential changes in the {{IsADAuthenticatedWithARCOSLogin}} webdtcore api to handle data overflow cases were absent. As a result, for newly added domains, the sas_id generated was of datatype long , while the api was handling the field as integer causing out of bounds error.

The following changes were made

# variable for sad id was converted from int to long in {{ArcosCommandSelectParameter}} model.
# Changed GetInt32Value to GetInt64Value while assign {{sad_id}} value for {{ArcosCommandSelectParameter}} object.

Additionally, we discovered that while calling the SP {{usp_CreateUpdateNewUser}}, the password is sent in encrypted format. However, the encryption method used ({{ARC_CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText_XWD()}}) was throwing exception. The concerned SP stores the password in {{ssu_password}} field in {{sso_users}} table. We had previously confirmed from the password vault team that {{ssu_password}} field should store values encrypted using {{CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText()}} method.
The following changes were made

# Change of encryption method from  {{ARC_CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText_XWD()}} to {{CommonFunctionsUtils.EncryptText()}} for calling the SP {{usp_CreateUpdateNewUser}}

The changes have been pushed to airtel & devint branch awaiting deploymentģ

FYI [~accountid:5e17c137800be30d9fba9377] [~accountid:712020:b51a19b9-8779-46c4-9ca5-48c40f7d0e56]

column1: Bank Of Jordan | ICBS Business Application SSO not working
column36:
We are in the process of configuring auto login for the *ICBS application*, where the username and password fields are enclosed within a text frame. Due to this, we are currently unable to inject credentials using a straightforward approach.

As a temporary workaround, we have configured the connector file using the {{<val>admin}} tag along with the {{sendkey}} method. While this allows credential injection, it results in the username field being populated with the static value ōadminö, as defined in the digital asset parameters during SSO.

Unlike PAM, where we have the flexibility to create multiple parameters for different services, the *CI module allows parameter configuration only at the Digital Asset level*, not at the Digital Identity level.

We are looking for a resolution that enables CI to inject credentials during SSO using the *username associated with the Digital Identity*, rather than a hardcoded value in the asset parameters.



I have shared the JSON file and html of ICBS application over email to Rahim
column340: Configured JSON with Selenium via textframe.

column401:

column402:
06-13-2025 12:52 PM;712020:06214b0f-55f3-423b-9f82-a18deef7fece;The issue with the ICBS application's SSO configuration has been successfully resolved after modifying the JSON file to align with the SSO process requirements.

Initially, the problem occurred because the JSON was created using the Automation Connector Builder Tool, which did not include the necessary CLI steps. As a result, the credential injection failed during the auto-login process.

Upon adding the valid CLI steps manually to the JSON file, the connector began functioning as expected, and the SSO process was completed successfully.
