column1: All application attributes in O365 are mandatory even if its not on target
column36:
All app attributes in O365 are mandatory even if O365 doesnt need these. The min attributes required for O365 are userPrincipaName, displayName, mailNickName. 

But we have all these mandatory on CI, very bad design and its quite wrong to ask customers to start using these fields even if they don't. Quite frustrating too as it doesn't really say required or not as there is no mandatory * there.

When I met Aniket on this, he said the API doesnt allow null values, we can pass the json without the field itself but cannot pass NULL. So, cant this be handled in product saying omit the fields which either not mapped or NULL?

Kashyap.

!image-20250609-053556.png|width=1043,height=462,alt="image-20250609-053556.png"!
column401:
06-12-2025 6:08 PM;712020:03d4db42-d8f8-4fb4-b7f6-b8dc74aaad4f;!image-20250612-123442.png|width=927,height=512,alt="image-20250612-123442.png"!



As discussed with you [~accountid:712020:32011361-c061-46e7-8f5d-a2098d2633a2] , O365 has its own way of handling Mandatory and Non-mandatory attributes where if the attribute is not required, it expects us to not put that attribute in API request body but if it is there, it expects some value and cannot be stored null.

 So, there has been cases where we have provided the connector with limited set of attributes but then got a request that why we are not able to set City, State or Country for target user on O365. After this, we have been told from the Product team that we should add all the possible fields that the target application provides making our connector standard, otherwise we will have to update the same application for every client and requests which will only increase the rework on the same connector.

Regarding showing mandatory and asterisk sign, I guess it is under development.

[~accountid:712020:f4f45d3f-4620-4fc0-9be0-6c4e5beab8fd]  [~accountid:5e17c137800be30d9fba9377] FYI
column402:
06-13-2025 7:54 AM;712020:32011361-c061-46e7-8f5d-a2098d2633a2;[~accountid:712020:03d4db42-d8f8-4fb4-b7f6-b8dc74aaad4f] I get it. I saw what you said. But am a little surprised this cannot be handled within the code?

IF there is NULL value then OMIT the attribute tag altogether so the API wont reject. Don't send NULL but omit the whole attribute. I have done these many times in past hence my failure in understanding the challenge.

column340: Implement SSO via ABE by setting up a proxy and providing the following parameter tags: <PROXY>.
column401:
06-12-2025 5:54 PM;712020:08760b73-b2e9-4bf2-9d94-0f4ca33c2362;RCA:

The Zendesk Connector supports SSO via ABE only. In this instance, by providing the parameters tags in field 4: <AUN><AUP><ASF><NATIVE><CUE>, SSO is being attempted via AWB-RPA. As a result, the SSO is failing.

Solution:

As it has been mentioned in the SOP document. Implement SSO via ABE by setting up a proxy and providing the following tags: <PROXY>.

Specifically, the SOP does not provide guidance or examples for the following fields required during configuration:

* Jira User API
* Access Token
* Group URL
* Get User URL

The absence of details for these fields made it difficult to proceed with confidence. Including clear instructions or example values in the SOP would greatly enhance our understanding and streamline the onboarding process.

Furthermore, after completing the configuration, we attempted to reconcile the asset. Unfortunately, the reconciliation failed and returned the following error:

{quote}ōError reading JArray from JsonReader. Path ''.ö{quote}

This error seems to indicate an issue with the expected JSON structure, potentially caused by incorrect or incomplete API configurations.

We would appreciate it if you could review the SOP for completeness, particularly with regard to the fields mentioned above, and help us understand the root cause of the reconciliation error. Any additional documentation, sample configurations, or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated.

column403:
07-01-2025 6:35 PM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;Hey, in CI there is a use-case that is missed by the product team, the feature is missing in workspace.

*In PAM -* 

For App Unix GUI, when we launch a service, we get aĀ drop-down to select what app we need to launch ahead. Also, the preference path is fetched based on the selection you make in this box.

!image-20250701-121927.png|width=609,height=212,alt="image-20250701-121927.png"!

{noformat}    { "0", "Xbrowser for Xmanager (XDMCP)", "xmanager.exe", "- Xmanager", "-query ", "", "0"},
    { "1", "Xming", "Xming.exe", "Xming -", "-query ", "", "50"},
    { "2", "VNC", "viewer.exe", "- TightVNC Viewer", "-optionsFile=", "", "65"},
    { "3", "XStart", "Xstart.exe", "", "", "Xstart", "126"}};{noformat}

The ids of these options are present in the last element, for example Xstart has id 126, so the connector queries the sso_users_preference table by specifying the user id and the id selected above and in return gets the preference path.

Below is the snapshot of the database -

!image-20250701-124635.png|width=981,height=399,alt="image-20250701-124635.png"!

where 35 is the userid and 65 is the preference id. 

Also, In the ACMO page we also see 3 options for App UNIX GUI with the same logic. 

!image-20250701-123802.png|width=1058,height=464,alt="image-20250701-123802.png"!

!image-20250701-123819.png|width=880,height=305,alt="image-20250701-123819.png"!

!image-20250701-123926.png|width=912,height=486,alt="image-20250701-123926.png"!

!image-20250701-123947.png|width=944,height=438,alt="image-20250701-123947.png"!



!image-20250701-124121.png|width=944,height=438,alt="image-20250701-124121.png"!

So, whenever I update the AppUnix GUI ( tnviewer.exe) option with the preference path in the preference page, the record in the sso_users_preference  having the current user-id and preference id as 65 gets updated with the preference path, similarly, if I select the type as App Unix GUI ( Xstart ) then the record with preference id as 126 and user id as current user id gets updated  with the preference path entered.

*In CI -*

We do not get 3 options for App Unix GUI in workspace, we only get 1 option that picks up the default id as 50. So whenever you update the path for AppUnix GUI, the path is stored with id as 50. 50 preference  id corresponds to the {{"Xming"}} option in App Unix GUI. So, whenever we launch the connector, and if we select VNC, it looks for its corresponding preference id that is 65, and queries the sso_users_preference  table with the user_id and preference id, since the workspace always updates the record with id as 50 by default, record with id as 65 is always empty, because of which the connector fails to fetch the value.

We updated the preference path in workspace and queried the database, check the snapshot below.

!image-20250701-125641.png|width=878,height=465,alt="image-20250701-125641.png"!



[~accountid:712020:b5be67fa-c999-4d3e-98ad-0adac11564be] , [~accountid:63510754c97f5473af6eb617]  please have a talk with the product team and make the changes in the workspace. 

++ [~accountid:62de82072fe585febb3b66ce] , [~accountid:712020:b51a19b9-8779-46c4-9ca5-48c40f7d0e56] , [~accountid:712020:00f8bef2-5f79-4480-aebe-9b575c155563] , [~accountid:5d133892a232e50bd6f277cf] .

column1: CUB | Dynatrace Application Integration with ARCON IDAM CI
column36:
Dear Team,

As discussed with CUB, we can proceed with integrating the Dynatrace application with IDAM CI. Dynatrace is an API-based application that supports SAML, but it is not integrated with AD and does not have a UAT environment. We have reviewed the possible pros and cons with CUB regarding the integration, and they have confirmed that we can move forward with the application integration.

column1: Requested SDP OEM to provide API details for processing integration with IDAM CI
column36:
Discussed Points:

# *Modification/Disable API Requires User ID*

Issue: The API for modification/disable requires a user ID, but the user ID is absent in the response of any existing API.
Discussion: It was emphasized that the user ID must be included in the response body.
Action: The SDP team acknowledged the issue and will work to resolve it with additional time.

# *Get All Users API to Fetch All AD Users*

Requirement: The system must retrieve all Active Directory (AD) users for full reconciliation purposes.
Discussion: The SDP team agreed to investigate this requirement further.
Action: The SDP team will provide updates on their findings.

# *Single API for Changing User Roles*

Requirement: A single API should be implemented to handle role changes between ōUserö and ōTechnician,ö eliminating the need for two separate APIs.
Action: The SDP team will evaluate the feasibility of this change and share their insights.

column340:
On the 3rd, after the issue arose, the Workspace team reviewed the generated token and identified that the session ID was being entered incorrectly in the token, resulting in an "unauthorized" error. After discussions with Saurabh Kohli, Soumya, and Kalpesh, a logger patch was provided to Vahid for both the new framework (newconnector) and the old ACMO (Ci_10.08 branch).

On the 4th (Saturday), after deploying the patch in the internal CI environment, an error was observed in the old ACMO. The logger patch was reverted, and Vahid was instructed to host the old ACMO separately. Another logger patch was provided from the ci_developer branch, but errors persisted in ACMO.

The branch identified by DevOps also encountered errors. After further investigation with Kalpesh and Soumya, the correct branch was identified, resolving the ACMO error. The logger patch was then deployed in the internal environment.

On the 5th, logs from both the connector and ACMO confirmed that the session ID for token generation was correct. However, during video recording checks with Vahid, it was noted that video images for some users were not being created, and two additional issues were found in the video recording executable.

Following discussions with Anshu, a logger patch was deployed at the endpoint where JWT tokens are generated.

On the 6th, Soumya and Meet worked on the two video recording issues, and the Workspace team applied the logger patch for further log analysis.

On the 7th, Param, Soumya, Kalpesh, and Anshu reviewed common API and cache key logs, identifying a bug. This was subsequently worked on by both the Connector and Workspace teams on the 8th.

On the 9th, after analysing logs from the common API, connector, new framework, and ACMO, it was discovered that the connector and newconnector video recording executables were generating tokens simultaneously. This caused the second generated token to override the first, rendering the first token invalid and resulting in the 401 Unauthorized error.

To resolve this architectural issue, the module name is now concatenated with the session ID when generating tokens from both the connector and newconnector modules. A patch with this solution has been deployed in the internal CI environment and is currently under observation to confirm whether the 401 error has been resolved.

column1: Some users are getting video recorder error in internal CI
column36: Hey, it has been observed that a few users are not able to access their services, the connector exe is being killed because of error while vide recording.
column340: configuration and adding a single setting for all the lobs using ōALL LOBö fixed the issue.
column401: 01-31-2025 4:01 PM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;Will post an update soon, I am in the process of fixing this.
column402: 01-31-2025 5:03 PM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;Hey, I added a logger and we found out that we were getting null data ( like image file path, etc ). It was later found out that the configuration settings in the image orchestrator for that LOB ( ANB lob ) was not set. Changing the configuration and adding a single setting for all the lobs using ōALL LOBö fixed the issue.

column340:
Following issues were found while validating SSO:
--	Webdtcore was missing.
--	ARCOSCLients was missing.
--	Connector API was missing.
--	Webdtcore api was pointing to a blank folder in IIS.
--	The  deployment pipeline was commented for webdtcore due to which the folder was not pushed
--	Database had default values for configs for aps_id 434 & 433.
--	Issue in the Webdtcore while getting data.
--	Following SPs were found missing:
	--	SP ōusp_GetORSetServiceRefTemplateō was missing  on Titan database.
	--	SP "usp_GetORSetCriticalCommands" was missing  on Titan database.
	--	SP "usp_GetFromTableValues" was missing  on Titan database.
--	XAuth api was failing when called the GetAPIToken method.

All the above were resolved.

column1: Internal CI - Prevent User from taking Unmonitored Session.
column36: Hi, there were a few instances reported that the end users are still able to continue their session inspite of the video recorder exe throwing an exception and showing a message box that states that by some reason it could not record the session.
column204:
1. The scenario has been handled for windows.Same scenario can occur for MAC and LINUX as well.Please handle on these OS platforms as well
2. Zero comments in the code.Sequence of events is difficult to understand
column340:
# video-record exe directly kills all the connector executables if the connector exe does not gets killed via message passing.
# connector repeatedly checks if videorecorder.exe is active, if not then it kills itself.
# connector kills itself if there is any exception while launching the video-record exe.
column389:
# Launch a service.
# If we get an exception from video recorder then the connector session should get killed.
# Also, if we try to manually kill the video recorder exe from task manager then too the connector session should get disconnected.
# Basically there should be no state that the user is able to continue the session and the logs are not getting generated.
column401:
01-21-2025 7:03 PM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;To find the RCA, we observed all the session id folders that were missing in the  image log folder, then we tried to call the person who took that sesison and checked if the session is still on.

We found 2 such cases today, we have added logs in the areas which we doubt to be problematic, those logger patches have been deployed on their local machine too. Will post an update if any.

column1: Prevent User from continuing the session if video recording stops.
column36:
When using RDPS, the video record exe monitors the other connector executables.

The use case is to test if there is no chance for the user to continue the session without being monitored. To test this we can kill the video record exe when the  user session is active, In case of any exception the error message is displayed to the end user, the user should not be able to ignore it and continue  the session.
column340: If in case of exception, the video record exe will send the message to the connector exe, to shut down and retry this for 3 times. This code is there in branch 35.8.23_hf already.
column389:
We tested the following test cases  : 
1. Killing the video record exe when the session is active. The user session got disconnected with the error message ōunable to record sessionö.
2. In case of an exception from the video recorder exe, show a message and terminate the user session. For this we manually generated the exception and tested it, the user session gets disconnected with the error message.
3. Tried the something multiple times.

column340:
Issue Summary:
We have integrated SAML authentication for the SDP application, but users are encountering an authentication error with code 53.

Possible Causes:
Misconfigured SAML Metadata ¢ Incorrect settings in the metadata file may be causing issues with authentication.
Invalid Certificates ¢ Expired or incorrectly configured certificates could be preventing a successful handshake.
Attribute Mapping Issues ¢ The required attributes might not be mapped correctly between the Identity Provider (IdP) and Service Provider (SP).
Client-Side Internet Connectivity Issues ¢ Network instability on the clientÆs side may be interrupting the handshake process between the IdP and SP.
Next Steps for Escalation:
Log Analysis: Review authentication logs to identify specific failures in the SAML request/response.
SAML Request & Response Validation: Ensure that the SAML assertions, attributes, and responses are correctly formatted and contain the expected data.
Metadata Configuration Review: Cross-check the IdP and SP metadata files to ensure they match the expected configurations.
Certificate Verification: Confirm that the certificates used for authentication are valid and properly configured.
Network Connectivity Check: Ensure that clients' network stability is not interfering with the SAML authentication process.
Urgency:
Immediate attention required ¢ This issue is blocking user access to the application.

Next Actions:
The issue needs to be escalated to another developer for further analysis and resolution. A prompt investigation is required to restore authentication functionality.
column389:
We have integrated SAML for the SDP application but are encountering an issue where users see an error with code 53 during authentication. This issue is likely related to misconfigured SAML metadata, invalid certificates, or attribute mapping. Additionally, there could be a potential internet connectivity issue on the client side affecting the handshake process between the Identity Provider (IdP) and Service Provider (SP).

We need to escalate this to another developer for further analysis. It is essential to review the logs, SAML request/response, and metadata configurations. The developer should also confirm that the clientÆs network stability is not interfering with the SAML authentication process. Immediate attention is required as this is blocking user access.

column408:
01-03-2025 1:47 PM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;Hello Viraj, if we add the global setting for clipboard then one more scenario we will need to handle in CI, that is has been handled in PAM recently.

Ā

In PAM, at user-level there are three options for a command - 1. enabled 2. disabled 3. Global. By default it is set to global.Ā

Ā

In CI, at user-level we only have 2 options for a command - enabled or disabled. *This was how PAM was working in older versions*.Currently there is no global setting, which makes it clear that for a particular user the command should be enabled or disabled, but Ā if we add a global level setting, then there comes an ambiguity, suppose if the global setting is on, and currently by-default the user is not granted clipboard command then how will we recognize that the clipboard was intentionally disabled for this user or no.Ā

Ā

In PAM the third option resolves this ambiguity, do we need similar approach in CI or no please have a thought about this and let us know.

column439:
05-20-2025 5:28 PM;712020:3e914ea0-e076-4b1e-a081-aea9ea7fff27;Hi [~accountid:5e17c137800be30d9fba9377] & [~accountid:712020:e94b6f5f-bd51-478c-aad4-ef6a069b850c] ,

We need to address two issues in the SOT connector:

----

# *Scan Behavior Issues:*

* *Initial Scan Failure:*
During the first-ever scan, if the SOTSCAN fails, the logs show a "Failed" error. However, when the scan is triggered again, it runs incrementally instead of performing a full scan.
* *Incremental Scan Failure:*
In cases where an incremental scan fails due to an error, the connector performs the next scan based on the timestamp of the failed attempt rather than the last successful scan.
This results in data loss.

----

# *Eliminating the Email Modification Logic:*

As per our email generation policy:
If a user has an email coming from SOT, it is retained. If not, an email is generated based on the policy.

Once a new email is generated, we send a communication to HR, who then manually enters the email in the HRMS. The SOT connector picks up this field and attempts to onboard or modify the user again Ś which results in failure.

To resolve this, we need to *eliminate email modification logic* in the connector. It should only handle email assignment during *initial user onboarding*.

01-21-2025 1:30 AM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;Hey, following is an update for this ticket.

The main issue was that the sign in automation was not working on the client's machine. But it was working in our internal environment, we did sent some incremental logger patches as we progressed towards closing this issue, since the issue was not getting replicated at our end.

We made some enhancements in the sign in automation, to ensure that the automation does not break at any point.

Following are the enchantments done -

#  In mobaxterm we used to directly hit a combination and open the new connection window, but sometimes it failed, so we made a GUI interaction and then hit the key combination to open the new connection window.
# Instead of seperately writing IP and checking the checkbox of username followed by entering the username in username field, we entered the IP address as username@ip address, this reduced a few automation steps, thus reducing failure points.
# Also, after entering the IP, username and port, we used to hit the enter key, which didn't work at the client's environment. So we implemented a robust approach to click on the "ok" button via GUI Automation.
# Entering the password is more secure now, since we do not relay on image comparison, we relay on the control of the console and enter the password.

Note - in the client's environment the host machine was a server in which already another user was logged in simultaneously and that user  launched mobaxterm. So whenever the current logged in user launched mobaxterm, the connector would query to get all mobaxterm processeses and would get two processes - one which the current user launched via connector, and other launched by the other logged in user. When we launch multiple mobaxterm Processes from the same user account then all get merged into one, hence in connector the code was written such that we only fetch the first mobaxterm processes, assuming that only one mobaxterm instance would be running ( others get merged with the old running mobaxterm process). However, we saw a new case in the client's environment which we need to handle.

column402:
01-04-2025 2:51 PM;62d14731bb346bdf82fb1119;Hey, following is the actual problem statement.

# I have 2 PAM environments deployed.
# If I launch a service from deployment A, then the video record exe will get the MS API details of deployment A and the process details to monitor, the next time I launch any service we first check if the videorecord exe is on or no, if it already on then we only pass the process details.
# Now I switch to deployment B, and then launch the service, it will only pass the process details to videorecord and not the MS API details, it will not record the service launched by deployment B.

Now, this is not a bug. The video record exe is NOT coded to support multiple environments parallel. There is no code to handle this. We will have to analyze the code and its impact first.

column340:
On the 3rd, after the issue arose, the Workspace team reviewed the generated token and identified that the session ID was being entered incorrectly in the token, resulting in an "unauthorized" error. After discussions with Saurabh Kohli, Soumya, and Kalpesh, a logger patch was provided to Vahid for both the new framework (newconnector) and the old ACMO (Ci_10.08 branch).

On the 4th (Saturday), after deploying the patch in the internal CI environment, an error was observed in the old ACMO. The logger patch was reverted, and Vahid was instructed to host the old ACMO separately. Another logger patch was provided from the ci_developer branch, but errors persisted in ACMO.

The branch identified by DevOps also encountered errors. After further investigation with Kalpesh and Soumya, the correct branch was identified, resolving the ACMO error. The logger patch was then deployed in the internal environment.

On the 5th, logs from both the connector and ACMO confirmed that the session ID for token generation was correct. However, during video recording checks with Vahid, it was noted that video images for some users were not being created, and two additional issues were found in the video recording executable.

Following discussions with Anshu, a logger patch was deployed at the endpoint where JWT tokens are generated.

On the 6th, Soumya and Meet worked on the two video recording issues, and the Workspace team applied the logger patch for further log analysis.

On the 7th, Param, Soumya, Kalpesh, and Anshu reviewed common API and cache key logs, identifying a bug. This was subsequently worked on by both the Connector and Workspace teams on the 8th.

On the 9th, after analysing logs from the common API, connector, new framework, and ACMO, it was discovered that the connector and newconnector video recording executables were generating tokens simultaneously. This caused the second generated token to override the first, rendering the first token invalid and resulting in the 401 Unauthorized error.

To resolve this architectural issue, the module name is now concatenated with the session ID when generating tokens from both the connector and newconnector modules. A patch with this solution has been deployed in the internal CI environment and is currently under observation to confirm whether the 401 error has been resolved.

* video log setting is disabled at service level (DI & DA)
* RDPS service was restarted by [~accountid:623052d13eacc50071fce900] 
* The RTSM setting was disabled in the settings page
* Also the video logs and delta images both the configurations were set at different locations.
* On accessing the above mentioned locations the folders did not have appropriate rights to access and create images into it.

Just wanted to highlight that this is a pure configuration on the CI product and it will work only if done properly. If this issues occur again I would request anyone who is validating this issue please check these configuration and then make an attempt to validate. This issue was closed a month back and came back to us today morning and we have fixed it by only checking the configuration and correcting them.

column1: Deutsche Telekom || O365 Digital identities customization
column36:
Hello Team,

The client has 2 lakhs+ DIs present on the O365 application which includes guest accounts and internal members accounts as well as present in the client environment. Now, the client has raised the requirement of not fetching all 2 lakhs + identities but based on the security groups that are defined in the O365 application.

Please find the below-mentioned requirements from the client side.

# Include all Internal M365 users ../. for everything... entitlement, dormancy, Recertifiacte, recon

Ā

# Exclude all users/Guest with: Ā[http://t-mobile.com|http://t-mobile.com|smart-link]  domain / Department : TMUS / Company Name T-Mobile US

Ā

# Include all other Guest/External members : With specific Security Groups membership

Ā

2nd Option:

Ā

DTDL update a Custom Atrbiute for all Guest Accounts (who are member of these security groups/entitlement) Ā: IAM Managed

Ā

And then IAM imports these identities based on the Custom Attribute filter.... along with the T-mobile filter

Ā

Kindly check and confirm the feasibility of the requirements mentioned above at the earliest. The affected milestone is the 10.05.002 version.

Kindly refer to the video recording for reference.

column1: Deutsche Telekom | Unable to fetch all the Digital Identities for O365. Only 100 are getting pulled
column36:
Hi Team,
We have on boarded O365 connector in DTs Converged Identity Solution. We performed the partial recon and pulled the entitlements. When we are performing Full Recon to get the DIÆs. Only 100 Digital identities are getting fetched. 


This is the result of server-side pagination, which returns 100 data per page by default. So only the first 100 users are getting fetched in CI. Even when we tried to test the API calling in Postman , we are getting 100 users only are a response.

We need to figure out a way to pull all the users from O365 and bypass pagination issue.

column403:
08-13-2024 12:28 PM;712020:7d8f817a-d49b-45f0-a6a5-e9ffcc427402;Hello Team,

PFA the BRS
Below are our understanding:
The client is using ADManager Plus, they have integrated there AD and Exchage server with it.

So, basically they use ADmamager Plus to create a user on AD and they select a template for the user to fill in its details. Once the creation is done, they will select on the next tab on the ADmanager that says exchange, where in they select the specific user and then from the drop down selects the database to be mapped to.

ĀOnce the email is assigned they, go in mobile services/protocol options in the admanager and check enable and disable for the Outlook application.

* There is other scenario also, wherein the user created in the AD manually and then that user is assigned then mail on the exchange server.
Where in the drop down of the list of databases appears and they select one as per there convience
* ĀFor deboarding/deprov

# The user should not be completed deleted, they just want the user to go in there disabled OU.
They wont be using grace period functionality.
# To deprov on the exchage, they simply click on the user and select disable option.
# Upon selecting its Outlook rights are pulled out too.
